headline: Second Nature Issues Allergy Alert for Keto Crunch Smart Mix Due to Undeclared Tree Nuts and Cherries

image.caption: Second Nature Issues Allergy Alert for Keto Crunch Smart Mix Due to Undeclared Tree Nuts and Cherries

description: Second Nature recalls Keto Crunch Smart Mix due to undeclared cashews, pistachios, and cherries that pose allergy risks. Distributed nationwide in 10-ounce pouches.
keywords: May contain undeclared Cashews, Pistachios, and Cherries

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Second Nature Brands has issued a recall for their Keto Crunch Smart Mix 10-ounce pouches because they may contain undeclared cashews, pistachios, and cherries, which are allergens not listed on the label.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The product involved in this allergy alert is the Keto Crunch Smart Mix by Second Nature Brands, packaged in a 10-ounce pouch with a UPC code of 077034013405. This product was distributed nationwide through retail stores and online orders.

The reason for the recall is the potential presence of undeclared allergens including cashews, pistachios, and cherries. These ingredients are tree nuts and fruit that can cause severe allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. The affected packages are specifically marked with a “Best if used by 2/12/2027” date on the backside of the pouch. Other packages with different best by dates are not affected.

As of the announcement on May 1, 2026, no illnesses or allergic reactions linked to this issue have been reported.

facts.reason: May contain undeclared Cashews, Pistachios, and Cherries

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check the packaging to identify if their product is affected by looking for the following details:

  • Product name: Keto Crunch Smart Mix
  • Package size: 10-ounce pouch
  • UPC code: 077034013405
  • "Best if used by" date printed on the backside of the pouch: 2/12/2027

Only products matching all these identifiers are affected. Packages with other best by dates are not subject to this recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have the affected Keto Crunch Smart Mix pouch, do not eat the product. Contact Second Nature Brands for instructions on product return or disposal. The company can be reached by phone at (800) 651-7263 or by email at recall@secondnaturebrandsus.com.

Individuals with allergies who have consumed this product and are experiencing any symptoms should seek immediate medical attention.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Undeclared allergens pose serious health risks to people with allergies or severe sensitivities. Consuming products containing cashews, pistachios, or cherries without this information on the label can lead to severe or life-threatening allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, which requires immediate medical attention.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Anyone who has purchased or received the Keto Crunch Smart Mix 10-ounce pouch by Second Nature with a "Best if used by 2/12/2027" date and UPC 077034013405 should check their product. This is especially important for individuals allergic to cashews, pistachios, or cherries.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Food allergies to tree nuts such as cashews and pistachios can trigger serious reactions, sometimes life-threatening. Cherries can also cause allergic responses in sensitive individuals. Accurate labeling is critical to help consumers avoid allergens. This recall highlights the importance of checking food labels carefully, especially for products marketed as specialty diets like keto mixes.

faq.question: What allergens are undeclared in the Keto Crunch Smart Mix?
faq.answer: The product may contain undeclared cashews, pistachios, and cherries, which are allergens for some consumers.

faq.question: Which packaging details identify the recalled Keto Crunch Smart Mix?
faq.answer: The affected product is a 10-ounce pouch with UPC 077034013405 and a "Best if used by" date of 2/12/2027 printed on the back.

faq.question: Were any allergic reactions reported due to this recall?
faq.answer: No allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported in connection with this product issue as of the recall date.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: This recall notice is based entirely on the announcement provided by Second Nature Brands and posted by the U.S. FDA on May 2, 2026. The FDA shares company announcements as a public service without independent review or endorsement. Consumers should consult the original FDA notice or product packaging for verification.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.

headline: a2 Milk Company Recalls a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula 0-12 Months USA Label for Possible Cereulide Toxin Contamination

image.caption: a2 Milk Company Recalls a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula 0-12 Months USA Label for Possible Cereulide Toxin Contamination

description: The a2 Milk Company recalls specific batches of a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula USA label due to cereulide toxin presence, posing potential health risks for infants.
keywords: a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula 0-12 months USA label
Presence of cereulide toxin produced by some strains of the bacterium Bacillus cereus

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The a2 Milk Company has voluntarily recalled three batches of its a2 Platinum Premium infant formula labeled for the USA market due to contamination with cereulide, a heat-stable toxin from Bacillus cereus, which may cause gastrointestinal illness especially in infants.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The a2 Milk Company (a2MC) announced a voluntary recall on May 2, 2026, for three specific batches of the a2 Platinum Premium USA label infant formula designed for infants aged 0-12 months. This milk-based powdered formula contains iron and was distributed only in the United States via a2MC’s own website, Amazon, and Meijer stores as part of the Operation Fly Formula initiative. Importation rights expired on December 31, 2025, and the product was discontinued and removed from sale before the recall.

The recall was initiated after additional testing revealed the presence of cereulide, a toxin produced by some Bacillus cereus strains, likely introduced through an ingredient in the formula. The three affected batches total 63,078 units, with approximately 16,428 units sold to consumers. The affected batches should not be used despite no confirmed illnesses.

Consumers who purchased the formula are advised to check the batch number and expiration date on the bottom of the tins to identify affected products and stop using them immediately.

Product NamePackaging FormatBatch NumberUse By Date
a2 Platinium Premium infant formula 0-12 months Milk-based powder with Iron31.7oz tin22102694547/15/2026
a2 Platinium Premium infant formula 0-12 months Milk-based powder with Iron31.7oz tin22103246091/21/2027
a2 Platinium Premium infant formula 0-12 months Milk-based powder with Iron31.7oz tin22103217121/15/2027

facts.product: a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula 0-12 months USA label
facts.reason: Presence of cereulide toxin produced by some strains of the bacterium Bacillus cereus

rows: a2 Platinium Premium infant formula 0-12 months Milk-based powder with Iron
a2 Platinium Premium infant formula 0-12 months Milk-based powder with Iron
a2 Platinium Premium infant formula 0-12 months Milk-based powder with Iron

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To determine if your formula is affected, locate the batch number and expiration date labeled "Use By" on the bottom of the 31.7 oz tins. The affected batches are:

  • Batch number 2210269454 with Use By date 7/15/2026
  • Batch number 2210324609 with Use By date 1/21/2027
  • Batch number 2210321712 with Use By date 1/15/2027

Only tins matching these batch numbers and dates are subject to the recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have any of the recalled batches, immediately discontinue use of the formula. Consumers should either dispose of the product properly or return it to the place of purchase for a refund. If your infant shows symptoms such as nausea or vomiting after consuming this formula, contact a healthcare provider promptly.

For questions or concerns, consumers can contact the a2 Milk Company at 1-844-422-6455, Monday through Friday from 8 AM to 5 PM Eastern Time, or visit their website at a2platinum.com.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Cereulide is a toxin produced by certain strains of Bacillus cereus bacteria. It is heat-stable, meaning it is not destroyed by heating, including during formula preparation with hot water. Exposure to cereulide-contaminated food can cause gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea and vomiting, usually occurring within 30 minutes to six hours after ingestion and commonly resolving within 24 hours. Because infants have developing immune systems, they are more vulnerable to complications such as dehydration, which can require medical attention.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Parents and caregivers who have purchased the a2 Platinum Premium USA label infant formula 0-12 months from a2MC’s website, Amazon, or Meijer stores should check if their product is part of the recall. The recall specifically applies to three batches of the product as identified by batch numbers and “Use By” dates located on the bottom of each tin.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Infant formula contamination poses particular health risks due to infants’ sensitive and developing immune systems. Bacillus cereus can produce cereulide, a toxin that survives heat treatment and can cause rapid-onset gastrointestinal illness. Prompt recognition of symptoms and product withdrawal can prevent dehydration and more serious complications in infants.

faq.question: What is cereulide and why is it a concern in infant formula?
faq.answer: Cereulide is a toxin produced by some Bacillus cereus bacteria. It is heat-stable and can cause gastrointestinal illness such as nausea and vomiting, which may be especially dangerous for infants due to risk of dehydration.

faq.question: Which batches of a2 Platinum Premium infant formula are affected by the recall?
faq.answer: The recall affects three batches with these batch numbers and Use By dates found on the bottom of the tins: 2210269454 (7/15/2026), 2210324609 (1/21/2027), and 2210321712 (1/15/2027).

faq.question: What should I do if I have purchased the recalled a2 Platinum Premium infant formula?
faq.answer: Stop using the product immediately. You can dispose of it safely or return it to your place of purchase for a refund. If your infant shows symptoms like nausea or vomiting, contact a healthcare provider immediately.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: This safety information is based entirely on the company announcement posted by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration on May 2, 2026. The FDA does not endorse the product or the company but provides this recall announcement as a public service.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
